ul.li: Checksum Algorithm: 16-bit one's complement of the one's complement sum of the ICMP message starting with the ICMP Type.

Type 3 Code 4: Used for Path MTU Discovery. If a router cannot forward a packet because it is too large for the next link and the DF (Don't Fragment) bit is set, it sends this message. The MTU field at offset 0x06 indicates the MTU of that link.
Code 0: TTL (Time to Live) exceeded in transit.
Cache Locality: The standard 8-byte ICMP header fits within a single cache line (64B). In high-performance bypass stacks, ensuring original IP header data is pre-aligned can reduce L1 misses.
